vocab.meaning: một hạt muối bỏ bể (số tiền quá nhỏ so với tổng thể)
vocab.example: Spending fifty dollars on sandals is just a drop in the ocean compared to what I spend on rent every month.

vocab.word: cost an arm and a leg
vocab.meaning: rất đắt đỏ, tốn rất nhiều tiền
vocab.example: I rarely buy luxury brands because those high-end sneakers usually cost an arm and a leg.

vocab.meaning: tiền bạc không thành vấn đề (vì rất giàu)
vocab.example: If money were no object, I would probably have a massive collection of bespoke leather shoes.

vocab.meaning: trông rất tuyệt vời, sang trọng
vocab.example: You don't always need to spend a fortune; I've found budget shoes that make me look a million dollars.

vocab.word: money burns a hole in one's pocket
vocab.ipa: /ˈmʌn.i bɜːnz ə həʊl ɪn wʌnz ˈpɒk.ɪt/
vocab.meaning: có tiền là muốn tiêu ngay lập tức
vocab.example: As soon as I get my paycheck, money burns a hole in my pocket and I often head straight to the shoe store.

vocab.meaning: vung tay quá trán, tiêu vượt quá khả năng tài chính
vocab.example: I'm careful not to live beyond my means just to keep up with the latest footwear trends.

vocab.meaning: nhận lại giá trị xứng đáng với số tiền bỏ ra
vocab.example: I've worn these boots for five years, so I've definitely got my money's worth out of them.

vocab.meaning: rẻ như cho, cực kỳ rẻ
vocab.example: During the clearance sale, I managed to pick up some casual sneakers for dirt cheap.

vocab.meaning: ném tiền qua cửa sổ, lãng phí tiền bạc
vocab.example: Buying poorly made shoes is like pouring money down the drain because they fall apart so quickly.

vocab.meaning: tích cóp, để dành tiền cho mục đích cụ thể
vocab.example: I usually squirrel away a small amount of cash each month to buy a high-quality pair of trainers once a year.
